content: Hello, I recently started to look into Rust for Linux project. Sorry if this is a silly question. I'm now interested in how the rust compile process works in this project. I saw there were some libraries under rust directory, and there were some samples using these libraries under samples/rust. I tried to read Makefile, but couldn't understand how they linked without cargo. Could anyone explain how it works?

Yes, I understood how I can run the build command in this project. I succeeded to build and run this project with QEMU. So my question is about the structure of this project. I also develop my hobby OS written in Rust, and I'm especially interested in how the custom alloc library are built in. (Maybe this kind of question is not suite for this stream?)
That link explains the build system to a degree for you. I guess since you're building your own OS you've got it figured out.
And from the link I sent you earlier https://docs.kernel.org/kbuild/makefiles.html#built-in-object-goals-obj-y
Hopefully this helps you piece things together.
Oh, I see. I couldn't find out the rule for rust files. I tried to search a rule, like *.rs or search the usage of rustc or cargo. Thanks for the information. That's really helpful!
Those rules live in scripts/Makefile.build -- found them via a quick git grep
Ooooh, yes. I should try git grep first. I tried to find these rules from some limited Makefiles, and gave up. Thanks.
